articles.headline: Union government tables bill to narrow definition of transgender persons
articles.summary: Social Justice Minister Virendra Kumar introduced the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026 in the Lok Sabha. The bill narrows the definition to those with biological variations and removes the right to self-perceived identity. It establishes medical boards for certification and prescribes up to life imprisonment for offences like kidnapping or forced mutilation of transgender children.

articles.headline: Maharashtra tables bill requiring 60-day notice for religious conversion
articles.summary: The Mahayuti government tabled the Maharashtra Freedom of Religion Bill to curb unlawful conversions. A key provision mandates a 60-day prior notice to district authorities for anyone intending to convert or organise a ceremony. Conversions involving coercion or fraud are prohibited, and family members are empowered to lodge FIRs against suspected unlawful acts.

articles.headline: PM Modi urges Assam voters to punish Congress for encouraging infiltration
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched development projects worth over ₹24,000 crore in Assam. In speeches at Kokrajhar and Guwahati, he accused the Congress of ignoring indigenous populations and encouraging illegal infiltration. Modi also criticized the opposition for spreading misinformation regarding the West Asian crisis and its impact on inflation.

articles.headline: Senior Maoist leader Devji calls for lifting ban on the outfit
articles.summary: Surrendered CPI (Maoist) leader Thippiri Tirupati, alias Devji, urged the government to recognize the group as a mainstream political party. He claimed the armed struggle has largely ended and requested the release of jailed sympathizers and leaders. Devji suggested the People’s Liberation Guerilla Army (PLGA) could be disbanded if the legal ban is lifted.

articles.headline: Andhra cabinet approves allotment of 66,157 acres of prohibited lands
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh cabinet cleared 66,157 acres of land from the prohibited property list to facilitate industrial development. The move allows industries to mortgage properties for loans. The cabinet also approved the Chief Minister Employment Generation Programme with a ₹300 crore allocation to support 3,500 micro-entrepreneurs.

articles.headline: Opposition seeking Rajya Sabha debate on MEA and Commerce ministries
articles.summary: Opposition parties are pushing for detailed reviews of the external affairs and commerce ministries in the Rajya Sabha, citing the US-India trade deal and the West Asia war. While the government prefers discussing health and sports, a deadlock remains over the Demand for Grants schedule. Typically, both houses select five ministries each for intensive annual review.

articles.headline: Opposition MPs submit notice for removal of Chief Election Commissioner
articles.summary: In a first-of-its-kind move, 193 MPs from the INDIA bloc and other parties submitted notices to both houses seeking the removal of CEC Gyanesh Kumar. The notice lists seven charges including partisan conduct and mass disenfranchisement. The initiative marks a significant escalation in the opposition's challenge to the poll panel's functioning.

articles.headline: Rohit Pawar alleges data tampering of aircraft black box in Baramati crash
articles.summary: NCP (SP) MLA Rohit Pawar claimed that the owner of VSR Ventures is attempting to edit the flight data recorder (FDR) of the aircraft that crashed in Baramati, killing Ajit Pawar. He warned that any tampering with the black box will be detected during forensic audits. The Learjet 45 crash on January 28 is currently under federal investigation.

articles.headline: PM Modi flags energy supply risks to Iranian President Pezeshkian
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a phone conversation with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian regarding the escalating tensions in West Asia. Modi expressed concern over the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, which critical for India’s LPG and crude oil imports. Iran’s Ambassador to India stated that they are working to solve the problem and expect supply news shortly.

articles.headline: Odisha Congress MLAs housed in Karnataka resort ahead of RS polls
articles.summary: Eight Odisha Congress MLAs have arrived in Bengaluru and are staying at a resort due to concerns about potential 'poaching' attempts by the BJP ahead of Rajya Sabha elections. Congress leaders called it a precautionary measure, citing past incidents of engineering defections. The BJP rejected the allegations, calling it a reflection of the Congress party's internal weaknesses.

articles.headline: Tehran Seeks Brics Unity as India, Iran EAMs Hold Fourth Call
articles.summary: Foreign ministers of India and Iran held their fourth conversation since the West Asia conflict began, with Tehran seeking BRICS solidarity on the escalating crisis. The talks between S Jaishankar and Abbas Araghchi focused on regional stability and the safety of commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: PM Modi Links Nehru Stance on Korea War to Inflation
articles.summary: Addressing a program in Guwahati, PM Narendra Modi accused the Congress of spreading misinformation and compared the current economic situation to India during the Korean War. He cited historical records suggesting that Jawaharlal Nehru had similarly linked domestic inflation to international conflicts at that time.

articles.headline: Opposition Protests Over Cooking Gas Shortage Disrupt Lok Sabha
articles.summary: Proceedings in the Lok Sabha were repeatedly adjourned as Opposition members protested against domestic cooking gas shortages. Legislators demanded a discussion on the government's failure to ensure adequate supply of LPG cylinders, which has left many households and small businesses struggling across the country.

articles.headline: MHA to Review Security Protocols for 400 VIP Protectees
articles.summary: Following an attack on ex-J&K CM Farooq Abdullah’s convoy, the Ministry of Home Affairs has ordered a comprehensive review of security protocols for nearly 400 VIPs. The review will involve the CRPF, CISF, and NSG to assess threats and ensure adequate protection for high-profile individuals in sensitive regions.

articles.headline: Opposition Moves Motion in Parliament to Remove CEC
articles.summary: Opposition parties have submitted a formal motion in both the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha seeking the removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. The motion lists seven charges, including alleged partisan conduct and failure to ensure fair electoral practices during recent state assembly polls.

articles.headline: Congress Accuses BJP of Poaching Odisha MLAs with Big Offers
articles.summary: Karnataka Congress leader DK Shivakumar has alleged that the BJP is attempting to poach Odisha MLAs by offering them large sums of money. Shivakumar is currently hosting eight first-time Congress MLAs from Odisha at a resort near Bengaluru to prevent them from switching sides ahead of the Rajya Sabha elections.

articles.headline: BJP Considering Kurien and Chandrasekhar for Kerala Assembly Polls
articles.summary: The BJP Central Election Committee is reportedly considering fielding Union ministers George Kurien and Rajeev Chandrasekhar in the upcoming Kerala assembly elections. The party aims to strengthen its presence in the state by nominating high-profile leaders for key constituencies like Thiruvananthapuram.

articles.headline: Tehran to allow Indian ships through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: In a phone call with PM Modi, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian assured that India-flagged ships would have safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran also facilitated the return of nearly 100 Indian crew members from the ship IRIS Lavon. The move comes amid rising tensions and maritime attacks in the region. Modi described Iran as a strategic friend.

articles.headline: Congress moves 8 Odisha MLAs to Bengaluru resort
articles.summary: To prevent potential cross-voting and horse-trading ahead of the Rajya Sabha polls, the Congress party has moved eight of its Odisha MLAs to a resort on the outskirts of Bengaluru. The move follows similar tactics by other parties as competition for RS seats intensifies. The legislators will be flown back to Odisha just hours before voting begins.

articles.headline: Maharashtra proposes 7-year jail for forced religious conversion
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government has introduced the Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026, which proposes up to seven years in prison for conversions through coercion, fraud, or marriage. The bill requires individuals to provide 60 days' notice to district authorities before converting. Civil society groups have expressed concern that the law could be used to target inter-faith couples.

articles.headline: Internal quota dispute splits Karnataka Dalit ministers
articles.summary: Tensions have surfaced within the Karnataka cabinet over the implementation of internal quotas for Scheduled Castes (SCs). Ministers representing different sub-groups are divided on whether to follow a 15% or 17% reservation model. A special cabinet meeting on March 26 is expected to decide the final formula, amid threats of protests from various Dalit organizations.

articles.headline: Delhi Metro Pink Line’s Soorghat station remains closed to public
articles.summary: Despite the opening of the full Pink Line ring metro, the Soorghat station remains unusable as trains pass without stopping. Officials stated that the station lacks a usable entry or exit for commuters, making it inaccessible for now. The Pink Line is touted as the first full ring metro line in the capital.

articles.headline: Delhi Police order guard for LPG depots to prevent hoarding
articles.summary: Delhi Police cancelled routine leaves and ordered 24/7 deployment outside LPG godowns and warehouses. The measure follows instructions from the Ministry of Home Affairs to prevent black marketing and ensure security during potential supply shortages linked to the West Asia conflict. Officers have been told to monitor cylinder shops and refilling stations across the capital.

articles.headline: NHAI increases annual FASTag pass fee to ₹3,075 from April
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has hiked the annual FASTag pass charge by ₹75. Starting April 1, the new fee will be ₹3,075. The pass, introduced in 2025, allows for 200 trips or one year of travel for private non-commercial vehicles at designated toll plazas.

articles.headline: Telangana CM unveils Musi River rejuvenation and development master plan
articles.summary: Chief Minister Revanth Reddy announced a rejuvenation project for the Musi River to mitigate floods and provide green spaces. The first phase, estimated at ₹7,000 crore, focuses on a 55-km corridor and includes riverbed profiling and trunk sewer installation. A Singapore-led consortium has been appointed as the aggregate master planner for the urban infrastructure asset.

articles.headline: Twenty-eight Indian fuel vessels stranded near Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: India’s energy supply chain is under stress as 28 vessels, including 11 LPG and three LNG carriers, remain stranded due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The vessels are carrying approximately 2.15 lakh tonnes of LNG and 17.5 lakh tonnes of crude oil. Authorities are closely coordinating to manage the situation and ensure the safety of seafarers in the high-risk maritime zone.

articles.headline: Gujarat allows industries to use alternative fuels amid gas crunch
articles.summary: The Gujarat Pollution Control Board has granted a three-month relaxation allowing industries dependent on natural gas to switch to approved liquid fuels without new environmental approvals. This temporary measure aims to help units maintain production while fuel supplies are constrained by West Asia disruptions. Approved alternatives include coal, lignite, light diesel oil, and biogas.

articles.headline: PM Modi unveils projects worth ₹19,680 crore in Assam
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi dedicated infrastructure and energy projects worth ₹19,680 crore in Guwahati. Key projects include railway electrification, doubling of the Furkating-Tinsukia line, and cruise terminals. He also released the 22nd instalment of the PM-KISAN scheme for 9.3 crore farmers and distributed land pattas to tea garden workers in the state.

articles.headline: Lila Global acquires first GIFT City structured India-flagged VLCC
articles.summary: Maritime platform Lila Global has acquired the 'Lila Jamnagar', a very large crude carrier (VLCC), structured through GIFT City and registered under the Indian flag. This marks the first time a vessel of this scale has utilized the IFSC ship leasing framework. The move underscores GIFT City's emergence as a maritime finance hub and aims to strengthen India's energy transportation capabilities.

articles.headline: Air India activates contingency plans to maintain Westbound connectivity
articles.summary: Air India is rerouting European and North American flights through the Red Sea and Mediterranean to bypass closed Gulf airspace. The adjustments add 30-45 minutes to flight durations and require technical refuelling stops in Rome or Vienna. The airline is also evaluating new services to East Asian destinations like Osaka and Jakarta to sustain passenger volumes during regional disruptions.

articles.headline: Karnataka Identifies Potential Site for Ballari District Airport
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has identified a potential site for a new airport in Ballari district, located about 13 km from Kurugodu. Industries Minister MB Patil stated that if found feasible, the project would improve connectivity for the Kalyana Karnataka region and support local industries like steel and mining.

articles.headline: GMR Wins 10-Year Bid for Delhi Airport Cargo Terminal
articles.summary: GMR Airports has won the bid to manage the cargo terminal at Indira Gandhi International Airport in Delhi for a 10-year period. The win comes after the airport operator's previous contract with Turkish Airlines’ cargo unit was terminated, allowing GMR to take direct control of the terminal's operations.

articles.headline: Kalpataru Wins Rights for ₹1,400 Crore Mumbai Redevelopment Project
articles.summary: Kalpataru Limited has secured the redevelopment rights for a massive housing project in Mumbai's Andheri West. The project, spread over three acres, has an estimated gross development value of over ₹1,400 crore and will include seven residential towers with modern amenities and improved infrastructure.

articles.headline: Petroleum Ministry Urges Shift to Piped Gas Amid LPG Supply Concerns
articles.summary: The central government has acknowledged potential cooking gas supply issues due to the escalating West Asia crisis. While current stocks are sufficient, officials are pushing domestic and commercial users to shift to piped natural gas (PNG) where infrastructure is available. The move is intended to reduce dependency on imported LPG shipments passing through volatile maritime routes.

articles.headline: GBA carves Bengaluru into 50 new administrative divisions
articles.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) has restructured the city into 50 divisions and 150 sub-divisions to streamline civic administration. The new structure, effective from May 15, aims to improve the delivery of services like waste management and road maintenance. The reorganization follows the dissolution of the previous 198-ward BBMP structure.

articles.headline: MB Patil: Karnataka to develop independent civil aviation policy
articles.summary: Karnataka Large and Medium Industries Minister MB Patil announced that the state is drafting its own civil aviation policy. The policy will focus on developing regional airports in Ballari, Vijayapura, and Koppal to improve connectivity and boost industrial growth. The state aims to have at least two identified sites for every major city within the next decade.

articles.headline: FASTag Annual Pass for private vehicles to cost Rs 3,075
articles.summary: Starting April 1, the cost of the FASTag Annual Pass for private vehicles will increase to Rs 3,075. The pass allows for unlimited travel across 200 designated toll plazas nationwide. The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways stated that the revision is part of its annual update to maintain highway infrastructure. Users can renew the pass through the FASTag app.

articles.headline: Supreme Court takes suo motu note of illegal Chambal sand mining
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has taken cognizance of rampant illegal sand mining in the National Chambal Sanctuary. The bench noted that the activity poses a grave threat to endangered wildlife, including the gharial and Gangetic dolphin. The court has requested the Chief Justice to assign the matter to an appropriate bench to address the environmental crisis across MP, Rajasthan, and UP.

articles.headline: Supreme Court rejects plea for mandatory menstrual leave for women
articles.summary: A bench headed by CJI Surya Kant refused to entertain a PIL seeking a uniform national policy for mandatory menstrual leave. The court cited "practical realities," suggesting such a law might lead to workplace discrimination or lower hiring of women. The bench left the decision to the government to formulate a model policy framework through consultation with stakeholders.

articles.headline: Higher court scrutiny is not a reflection on judge's ability: HC
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court held that stay orders or modifications by higher courts on trial court decisions should not be seen as a negative reflection on the lower court judge's competence. The court clarified that judicial scrutiny is an inherent feature of the hierarchical court structure and essential for the legal system's functioning.

articles.headline: Supreme Court stays HC order on Lokpal nod in Moitra case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has stayed a High Court order concerning the Lokpal's authorization to the CBI to proceed in matters related to Mahua Moitra. The case involves ongoing legal scrutiny of legislative procedures and agency powers.

articles.headline: Supreme Court stays Telangana 90-day notice rule for film ticket hikes
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stayed a Telangana High Court order that required the state to notify film ticket price hikes 90 days before release. The plea was filed by Mythri Movie Makers, challenging an interim order that restricted immediate pricing decisions. The dispute stems from a January 8 memo permitting a price hike for a specific film release.

articles.headline: SC rejects Mizo Chief Council's absolute claim over Lushai Hills land
articles.summary: The Supreme Court settled a decade-old dispute by rejecting a plea from Mizo chiefs claiming ownership of all land in modern-day Mizoram. The bench ruled that the petitioners failed to provide conclusive proof of title. The court held that rights were legally vested in the state under the 1954 Acquisition of Chief's Rights Act, dismissing reliance on ambiguous British-era records.

articles.headline: Govt introduces amendment Bill to protect transgender persons from exploitation
articles.summary: The Union government introduced a Bill in the Lok Sabha to amend the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019. The legislation proposes rigorous life imprisonment and a minimum fine of ₹5 lakh for the forced conversion of children to a transgender identity. It also widens the legal definition of transgender persons to include socio-cultural identities and congenital variations.

articles.headline: CBI questions Jai Anmol Ambani in ₹228 crore bank fraud
articles.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) questioned Jai Anmol Ambani, son of industrialist Anil Ambani, regarding an alleged ₹228 crore fraud case involving Reliance Home Finance Ltd. The case, based on a complaint by Union Bank of India, involves charges of criminal conspiracy and cheating. Investigators examined Jai Anmol for over six hours at the CBI headquarters in New Delhi.

articles.headline: High Court allows WinZO to use frozen funds for employee salaries
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has allowed ZO Pvt Ltd, parent of WinZO Games, to partially defreeze accounts to pay salaries for 220 employees. The Enforcement Directorate had previously frozen ₹505 crore of the company's assets under the PMLA. The court ruled that salary payments were reasonable as the ED failed to clearly link the entire amount to a predicate offence or 'proceeds of crime'.

articles.headline: New Bill Redefines Transgender Persons and Excludes Sexual Orientation
articles.summary: A new bill introduced in the Lok Sabha aims to provide a clearer legal definition of transgender persons but specifically excludes sexual orientation from its scope. The legislation proposes graded punishments for crimes against transgender individuals, ranging from two years' imprisonment to life for certain offenses.

articles.headline: Adobe Agrees to $75 Million Settlement Over Subscription Fees
articles.summary: Adobe has agreed to pay $75 million to resolve a US lawsuit alleging that the company failed to clearly disclose high termination fees for its software subscriptions. The settlement will provide refunds to eligible customers who were charged hidden fees when trying to cancel their Creative Cloud memberships.

articles.headline: SC: Mandatory period leave may deter hiring of women
articles.summary: A Supreme Court bench headed by CJI D.Y. Chandrachud observed that mandating paid menstrual leave could potentially create a bias against women in the workplace. The court stated that such a move might discourage firms from hiring female employees, viewing them as less productive or more expensive. The court asked the government to frame a policy instead.

articles.headline: Transgender Bill drops controversial 'self-perceived identity' clause
articles.summary: In a major amendment to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Bill, the government has removed the clause allowing 'self-perceived gender identity'. Individuals must now obtain a transgender certificate issued by a district magistrate to be legally recognized. The move aims to prevent misuse while ensuring genuine beneficiaries receive legal protection.

articles.headline: Bank ordered to pay Rs 3.2cr for refusing Demonetisation deposit
articles.summary: The National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission has ordered a bank to pay Rs 3.2 crore plus 6% interest to a company after it refused to accept a cash deposit during the 2016 demonetisation period. The bank had labeled the deposit 'suspicious' without a valid KYC or regulatory reason. The court ruled that the bank's unilateral action was a deficiency in service.

articles.headline: Man loses Rs 2.3L in online rice transport scam
articles.summary: A 47-year-old Bengaluru man was swindled of Rs 2.3 lakh while trying to book transport for rice from Andhra Pradesh. The victim clicked on a suspicious link sent by a person posing as a representative of a well-known transport company. Within 10 minutes, the money was siphoned from his account. Police have registered a case under the Information Technology Act.

articles.headline: SC panel limits Hauz Khas park deer population to 38
articles.summary: A Supreme Court-appointed panel has determined that only 38 deer can be kept at the Hauz Khas park in Delhi. This move follows ongoing efforts to manage wildlife and ecological balance within urban park spaces.

articles.headline: Editorial: India must prepare for likely re-emergence of El Nino
articles.summary: Global weather watchers forecast an 80% chance of a strong El Nino this year, which could threaten India's agricultural output and spark food inflation. Previous El Nino years have led to drought conditions and significant contractions in agricultural growth. The IMD must provide a realistic assessment in its April forecast to enable proactive policy choices on water and trade conservation.

articles.headline: Parliamentary panel moots green ammonia push for fertilizer sector
articles.summary: A Parliamentary Standing Committee has urged the government to formulate a roadmap for using green ammonia in fertilizer production to reduce reliance on imported natural gas. This comes as urea imports are set to hit a record high in 2025-26. The panel suggested urea plant-wise targets and investment plans to support India's climate goals and ensure fertilizer supply security.

articles.headline: Gujarat to be predominantly urban by 2027
articles.summary: The Gujarat Economic Review 2025-26 estimates that the state's urban population will reach 50.69% by 2027, surpassing the rural population for the first time. The shift is attributed to rapid industrialization and expansion of city limits. The state’s total population is projected to hit 7.48 crore by 2027, making it one of India’s most urbanized regions.

articles.headline: Suspect in synagogue attack lost relatives in recent airstrike
articles.summary: Reports indicate that a suspect involved in an attack on a synagogue recently lost several family members in an airstrike. The development provides context to the ongoing conflict and motivations behind recent acts of violence.

articles.headline: Two Indians killed in drone strike in industrial area in Oman
articles.summary: A drone strike in the Al Awahi Industrial Area of Sohar, Oman, killed two Indian nationals and injured ten others. These represent the first Indian land fatalities in West Asia since the start of the Iran-US conflict on February 28. The Indian mission is coordinating medical care for the injured and the repatriation of the deceased.

articles.headline: Six US personnel killed as refuelling aircraft crashes in Iraq
articles.summary: A US KC-135 aerial refuelling aircraft crashed in western Iraq, killing all six crew members. While Iran-backed factions claimed to have downed the plane, the US military stated the crash was not caused by hostile or friendly fire. The incident brings the total number of US troops killed in the current conflict to 13.

articles.headline: Iran repatriates crew of IRIS Lavan warship docked at Kochi
articles.summary: Most crew members of the Iranian warship IRIS Lavan, which docked in Kochi last week, were repatriated via a chartered flight to Armenia. The flight also carried the bodies of sailors killed when the IRIS Dena was sunk by a US submarine on March 4. A limited number of personnel remain in Kochi to maintain the vessel.

articles.headline: US temporarily eases sanctions on Russian oil amid crude price surge
articles.summary: The US Treasury Department announced a 30-day waiver on sanctions for Russian oil shipments loaded by Thursday. The move aims to stabilize global energy markets after crude prices rose due to the Iran-US war. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated the waiver is a short-term measure to provide a "green light" for purchasers without violating sanction rules.

articles.headline: Blast rocks Tehran during Quds Day rally as Israel strikes 200 targets
articles.summary: A large explosion hit a central Tehran square during a state-organised rally, though no casualties were reported. Concurrently, Israel launched a massive wave of strikes on 200 Iranian targets, including missile launchers. In Washington, President Trump vowed to intensify strikes, claiming the campaign would only end when he "feels it in his bones."

articles.headline: Israeli strikes kill over 100 children in Lebanon; 800,000 displaced
articles.summary: The Lebanese health ministry reported that 773 people, including 100 children, have been killed since the latest conflict with Israel began. An Israeli strike in Sidon killed eight people on Friday. The UN has launched a $325 million flash appeal to support over 800,000 displaced citizens, warning that global energy price spikes could constrain international donor funding.

articles.headline: Six civilians killed in Pakistan airstrikes on Afghan border
articles.summary: Taliban authorities claimed Pakistani airstrikes in Kabul and Nangahar killed six people, including women and children. Pakistani sources stated they targeted four "terrorist camps" and an oil facility at Kandahar airport. The border clashes have intensified as Islamabad seeks to target Islamist extremists allegedly using Afghan soil for militancy.

articles.headline: Trump claims new Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei damaged
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump stated that Mojtaba Khamenei, the newly appointed Iranian Supreme Leader, was 'damaged' during the first day of the US-Israel war on Iran. Reports indicate Khamenei may have been lightly injured but continues to operate. He was selected by a clerical assembly following the death of his father, the former supreme leader, in a recent military strike.

articles.headline: UK: Russian intelligence aiding Iranian military tactics and capabilities
articles.summary: UK Defence Secretary John Healey stated that Russian intelligence is likely supporting Iran with satellite imagery and drone targeting tactics during the conflict with the US and Israel. Healey noted that the patterns of Iranian attacks reflect tactics used by Russia in Ukraine, suggesting a deepening alliance of aggression between Moscow and Tehran to help Iran target Western assets.

articles.headline: Dubai Financial Hub Disrupted as Debris Hits Office Building
articles.summary: Operations at the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) were disrupted after a building was struck by debris from an intercepted Iranian strike. Major global events, including the TOKEN2049 crypto conference, have been cancelled or postponed as the city deals with the impact of the regional conflict.

articles.headline: Tehran to Provide Safe Passage for India-Bound Vessels
articles.summary: Iran’s Ambassador to India has assured that Tehran will provide safe passage to vessels bound for Indian ports through the Strait of Hormuz. The move is intended to secure critical energy and trade routes for India despite the ongoing military tensions and naval blockades in the West Asia region.

articles.headline: Explosions Kill Over 200 at Pro-Palestine Rally in Tehran
articles.summary: At least 200 people were killed when a series of explosions rocked an annual pro-Palestine rally in Tehran. The attack, which Iranian officials have blamed on foreign agents, has significantly escalated regional tensions and prompted fears of an all-out war between Iran and Israel following recent drone strikes.

articles.headline: US Authorises More Russian Oil Sales to Curb Price Shock
articles.summary: The United States has issued a second authorisation allowing more Russian oil tankers to operate in international waters to prevent a global supply shock. The move aims to curb the rapid rise in fuel prices following the closure of major shipping routes in the Persian Gulf due to the West Asia conflict.

articles.headline: Saudi Arabia Cuts Oil Output by 20% After Offshore Strikes
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia has been forced to cut its oil production by 2 million barrels per day (20%) after drone strikes hit two of its major offshore oil fields. The production cut has sent global Brent crude prices soaring above $105 per barrel, threatening to severely impact energy-importing nations like India.

articles.headline: French Soldier Killed and Six Wounded in Iraq Drone Attack
articles.summary: A French soldier was killed and six others were wounded in an Iranian-made drone attack on a French military base in northern Iraq. French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the attack as 'unacceptable and unjustified,' adding that French forces would remain in the region to continue the fight against terrorism.

articles.headline: Iran Assures Safe Passage for Indian Vessels Through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran’s Ambassador to India, Mohammad Fathali, confirmed that Tehran will ensure safe transit for India-bound ships despite the regional conflict. The assurance comes as shipping data shows LPG carriers and crude tankers transiting the strait. Fathali cited 'friendly relations' and common interests as the reason for the move, helping ease concerns over India's energy security.

articles.headline: Trump aides divided over exit vs escalation in Iran
articles.summary: A rift has emerged within the Trump administration regarding U.S. strategy toward Iran. While some aides advocate for a total exit and easing of sanctions to stabilize global oil prices, others are pushing for military escalation following recent drone strikes on U.S. assets. The disagreement comes as global energy markets reel from supply threats in the Persian Gulf.

articles.headline: 2026 T20 World Cup sees record scoring rates fueled by boundaries
articles.summary: Analysis of the recently concluded men’s T20 World Cup shows batsmen reached a record average run rate of 8.41 per over. India led the tournament with a 9.7 scoring rate, amassing 67.5% of its runs through boundaries. The tournament saw the lowest share of dot balls in history (38.3%), reflecting an aggressive shift in batting tactics similar to recent IPL templates.

articles.headline: Safety works at Chinnaswamy Stadium reviewed ahead of IPL matches
articles.summary: The Karnataka State Cricket Association (KSCA) briefed a state-appointed expert committee on security upgrades at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium. The discussions focused on safety measures required to host Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) home matches following last year's stampede concerns. RCB is scheduled to play five home matches at the venue this season.

articles.headline: India defeats Italy 1-0 to reach World Cup hockey qualifiers final
articles.summary: The Indian women's hockey team defeated Italy in the semi-finals of the FIH World Cup Qualifiers in Hyderabad. Manisha Chauhan scored the winning goal via a penalty corner in the 40th minute. India will face England in Saturday's final, with both teams having already secured qualification for the 2026 World Cup.

articles.headline: Revised TOPS core group includes shooters Samrat Rana and Suruchi Phogat
articles.summary: The Ministry of Sports updated its Target Olympic Podium Scheme (TOPS) core list to include top performers from 2025. World champion Samrat Rana and pistol shooter Suruchi Phogat were added to the core group. Boxers like Jaismine Lamboria and runner Gulveer Singh were also upgraded, while several veterans like HS Prannoy and Annu Rani were excluded or moved to the Asian Games group.

articles.headline: Sportstar Aces Awards 2026 celebrates pioneers of Indian sports
articles.summary: The eighth Sportstar Aces Awards in Mumbai honoured the athletes who shaped the past year in Indian sport. The event highlighted the journey of women's cricket, honoring ten former captains including Jhulan Goswami and Diana Edulji. Weightlifter Mirabai Chanu and shooter Suruchi Singh were among top winners. Former Test captain Nari Contractor received the Lifetime Achievement award.

articles.headline: KKR Signs Zimbabwe's Blessing Muzarabani for 2026 IPL Season
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) have announced the signing of Zimbabwe’s fast bowler Blessing Muzarabani for the upcoming 2026 IPL season. Muzarabani replaces Allah Ghazanfar in the squad and brings significant experience from various T20 leagues, strengthening KKR's pace bowling attack for the tournament.

articles.headline: Pep Guardiola Warns Man City Title Bid Depends on Perfect Finish
articles.summary: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola has warned his players that any further points dropped in the Premier League will effectively end their title bid. With the title race entering its final weeks, Guardiola stressed that winning every remaining match is essential to beat rivals Arsenal and Liverpool to the trophy.

articles.headline: Sunrisers Hyderabad faces backlash over signing of Pakistani spinner
articles.summary: SRH is facing social media criticism after signing Pakistani spinner Abrar Ahmed for Rs 2.3 crore in the latest IPL auction. Fans have called for a boycott, citing the ongoing ban on Pakistani players in the league. However, BCCI officials clarified that Abrar was signed from a team based outside Pakistan (Sunrisers Leeds), which technically bypasses current restrictions.

articles.headline: Iran: No country can be excluded from FIFA World Cup
articles.summary: Following suggestions that the US might try to block Iran from the 2026 World Cup, Iranian football officials stated that FIFA regulations prohibit the exclusion of any qualified nation for political reasons. The tournament, co-hosted by the US, Canada, and Mexico, must remain open to all, Iran argued. FIFA has not officially commented on any potential ban.

articles.headline: Iran allows two Indian LPG ships to transit Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iranian officials permitted two Indian-flagged LPG carriers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, easing India’s cooking gas shortage. The move followed discussions between PM Narendra Modi and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian. While one crude tanker is nearing Mumbai, approximately 10 to 12 more vessels remain held up due to the ongoing West Asian conflict.

articles.headline: US and India nearing agreement on critical minerals deal
articles.summary: American envoy Sergio Gor announced that India and the US are close to finalizing a critical minerals agreement. The deal, expected in the coming months, aims to secure supply chains for advanced manufacturing and emerging technologies. This follows a series of breakthroughs in bilateral trade relations.

articles.headline: Sensex tanks 1,470 points as geopolitical tensions rattle markets
articles.summary: The BSE Sensex plummeted 1.93% to settle at 74,563.92, while the Nifty dropped 488 points. The three-day selloff has wiped out ₹33.6 lakh crore in market valuation. Escalating West Asia conflict and rising crude prices have triggered persistent foreign institutional investor outflows, with Larsen & Toubro and Tata Steel leading the losses.

articles.headline: Indian stock markets suffer worst weekly fall since pandemic crash
articles.summary: The Sensex shed 7,375 points (5.5%) and the Nifty lost 1,300 points (5.3%) this week, marking their worst performance since March 2020. Over ₹20 lakh crore in market capitalisation was wiped out in five sessions. The crash was driven by surging crude oil prices past $100 per barrel, the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and relentless selling by foreign institutional investors.

articles.headline: US initiates Section 301 forced labour probe against India
articles.summary: The US Trade Representative has launched a fresh Section 301 investigation against India and 59 other economies. The probe aims to determine if these nations failed to prohibit imports of goods produced with forced labour. New Delhi is currently examining the implications of this move on bilateral trade negotiations and exports, particularly in the textiles and apparel sectors.

articles.headline: DBS Bank: India better placed to absorb current oil surge
articles.summary: DBS Bank analysts suggest India has stronger macroeconomic buffers and more diversified crude sourcing than in previous cycles to manage high oil prices. While higher crude prices remain a risk to inflation and the current account deficit, the bank maintains that India remains an attractive long-term investment destination due to infrastructure and manufacturing growth prospects.

articles.headline: Agencies raise India inflation forecast as oil and rupee slide
articles.summary: Nomura, Barclays, and HDFC Bank have raised their inflation forecasts for FY27 due to rising crude prices and rupee depreciation. Retail inflation reached an 11-month high of 3.21% in February. Experts estimate that every $10 per barrel rise in oil prices could increase CPI inflation by approximately 100 basis points if indirect impacts through higher input costs persist.

articles.headline: Indian banks record robust growth in deposits and advances
articles.summary: Scheduled banks reported a jump in deposits by ₹4.20 lakh crore and advances by ₹3.24 lakh crore in the fortnight ended February 28. Experts attribute this to money moving from volatile stock markets into bank deposits and increased government spending ahead of state elections. Credit growth continues to surpass deposit growth on a year-on-year basis, standing at 15.19%.

articles.headline: SEBI caps mutual fund intra-day borrowings at 20% of assets
articles.summary: Market regulator SEBI has introduced a new framework allowing mutual funds to borrow up to 20% of a scheme's net assets for intra-day needs. The facility, effective April 1, is designed to help funds meet redemption pressures in debt schemes, equity-oriented index funds, and ETFs. Borrowings are restricted to a six-month tenure and require board approval for limits exceeding 20%.

articles.headline: Indices Mark Steepest Drop of Wartime Erasing ₹34 Lakh Crore
articles.summary: Indian stock markets plunged as the Sensex fell 1,470 points and the Nifty dropped 450 points, marking their sharpest single-day decline since the West Asia conflict began. Nearly ₹33.8 lakh crore of market capitalisation was wiped out in two weeks as crude oil prices stayed above $100 per barrel, triggering broad-based selling.

articles.headline: Govt Assures Cooking Gas Supply Amid Shift to Piped Gas
articles.summary: The Indian government has assured citizens that there is no shortage of cooking fuel but is encouraging a shift to piped natural gas (PNG) to reduce pressure on LPG cylinder supplies. The move follows delivery delays caused by the ongoing maritime conflict in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Economic Stabilisation Fund to Help Manage External Supply Shocks
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman announced a proposed ₹1 lakh crore Economic Stabilisation Fund to help the government respond to external shocks and supply chain disruptions. The fund aims to insulate the domestic economy from global price volatility and ensure the fiscal deficit target for FY26 is met.

articles.headline: House Panel Recommends Graded MDR for UPI Transactions
articles.summary: A parliamentary standing committee has recommended introducing a graded merchant discount rate (MDR) for UPI transactions. The move aims to ensure the financial sustainability of the digital payment ecosystem and allow banks and payment service providers to recover the costs of maintaining the massive UPI infrastructure.

articles.headline: IDBI Bank Strategic Sale Faces Delay as Bids Trail Reserve Price
articles.summary: The Indian government’s plan to sell its stake in IDBI Bank may face a delay as preliminary bids for the lender are reportedly lower than the internal reserve price. The government is seeking to raise approximately ₹30,000 crore from the sale but may have to wait for better market conditions before finalising the deal.

articles.headline: NARCL to Acquire ₹1,500 Crore Debt of Kurukshetra Expressway Project
articles.summary: The National Asset Reconstruction Company (NARCL) has emerged as the winner to acquire the ₹1,500 crore debt of the Kurukshetra Expressway project. NARCL offered ₹345 crore for the distressed asset, which had been stuck in litigation and insolvency proceedings for several years due to land acquisition issues.

articles.headline: RBI Injects ₹50,000 Crore Liquidity Through Open Market Operations
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has injected ₹50,000 crore into the banking system through open market operations (OMOs) to meet a sudden surge in liquidity demand. The shortage was triggered by quarterly advance tax payments and higher GST collections, which had tightened cash conditions in the interbank market.

articles.headline: Dalal Street Benchmarks Post Worst Weekly Fall in Six Years
articles.summary: Indian equity markets recorded their steepest weekly decline since May 2020, with the Nifty 50 falling 5.3% over the week. The Sensex declined 1,470 points on Friday. The sell-off was triggered by rising crude oil prices, consistent FII outflows, and geopolitical tensions in West Asia. The India VIX surged to 22.66, reflecting heightened investor anxiety over the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

articles.headline: Government Will Meet Revised Fiscal Deficit Target Despite Higher Spending
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman told Parliament that the government will stick to its 4.4% fiscal deficit target for FY26. To manage global headwinds like the West Asia crisis, ₹57,000 crore has been earmarked for an economic stabilization fund. Higher tax and non-tax revenues are expected to offset the ₹2 trillion in additional supplementary spending sought for subsidies and defense.

articles.headline: India and US Continue Engagement for Mutually Beneficial Bilateral Trade Pact
articles.summary: The commerce ministry has denied reports of a hold on trade discussions with the United States. Both nations remain engaged in talks to reach a bilateral trade agreement that serves mutual interests. The ministry emphasized that dialogue continues despite external global pressures and changing trade dynamics under various international administrations.

articles.headline: India Launches Anti-Dumping Investigation Against Industrial Chemical Imports from Taiwan
articles.summary: The commerce ministry has initiated an anti-dumping probe into phthalic anhydride imports from Taiwan following complaints from domestic manufacturers. The chemical is essential for producing plastics and paints. Domestic firms allege they are being priced out by a sharp rise in imports, despite an overall decline in India's total imports of the product.

articles.headline: Income Tax Department Nudges Reduced Refund Claims by ₹2,000 Crore
articles.summary: CBDT Chairperson Ravi Agrawal reported that five million tax returns were revised after the department flagged bogus or incorrect deduction claims. The 'nudge' strategy encouraged voluntary compliance instead of formal scrutiny. Tax refunds issued this year totaled ₹3.34 trillion as of February 10, approximately 19% lower than the same period in the previous fiscal year.

articles.headline: Foreign Investors Double Market Hedges as West Asia War Fuels Anxiety
articles.summary: FPIs have significantly increased short positions in Nifty and Bank Nifty futures to hedge against a potential market crash. Cumulative net short positions reached a record 260,540 contracts as the Iran-Israel conflict escalated. The rise in hedging signifies fear over high crude prices and supply chain disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Centre scraps IDBI Bank sale process after five years
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet has officially called off the disinvestment of IDBI Bank after bids received were lower than the reserve price. This move halts the process to raise up to Rs 80,000 crore in the next fiscal year. The decision follows a lack of interest from global investors due to strict regulatory conditions andvaluation concerns.

articles.headline: LPG crisis: Karnataka halts commercial supply for 10 days
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has announced a 7-10 day suspension of commercial LPG supply to hotels and industries due to a severe shortage. Nearly 30% of eateries across the state have scaled down operations or shut down. Domestic supply remains unaffected, but the government has urged residents to use electricity-based cooking to conserve stocks.

articles.headline: FSSAI makes food registration and licenses valid forever without renewals
articles.summary: Food regulator FSSAI announced that registration certificates and licenses will now have perpetual validity, eliminating the need for periodic renewals. The reform also includes increasing the turnover threshold for registration to ₹1.5 crore from ₹12 lakh. Street vendors registered with municipal bodies will now be considered deemed registered with FSSAI.

articles.headline: CBI questions Jai Anmol Ambani in ₹228-crore bank fraud case
articles.summary: The CBI questioned industrialist Anil Ambani's son, Jai Anmol Ambani, in connection with an alleged fraud involving Reliance Home Finance Ltd (RHFL). The probe follows a case registered in December 2024 concerning a ₹228-crore loss to Union Bank of India. Jai Anmol is a former director of the company named in the FIR.

articles.headline: Sugar industry seeks higher ethanol blending limits beyond 20%
articles.summary: The sugar industry has urged Food Minister Pralhad Joshi to increase ethanol blending targets beyond the current 20%. Industry representatives noted that higher blending would enhance national energy security, support farmers, and utilize significant under-utilised production capacity. The government indicated it will consider the proposal once current fuel supply pressures ease.

articles.headline: Auto industry body SIAM voices concern over West Asia conflict
articles.summary: The Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers (SIAM) stated that the ongoing conflict in West Asia remains a concern for supply chains and exports. Despite record domestic wholesales in February across passenger vehicles, two-wheelers, and three-wheelers, the industry is monitoring geopolitical developments that could impact manufacturing processes and component availability.

articles.headline: Gas shortages force steel mills to cut production by 50%
articles.summary: Ongoing supply disruptions of industrial gas have forced many steel companies, particularly in Gujarat, to cut production by up to 50%. The shortage of propane, LPG, and natural gas is adversely impacting manufacturing processes. Additionally, the sharp depreciation of the rupee has increased the cost of coking coal imports, further squeezing margins for long-steel producers.

articles.headline: Hospitality sector reports 30% hotel closures due to LPG shortage
articles.summary: Industry bodies estimate that nearly 30% of hotels and restaurants across major cities have temporarily suspended operations due to the LPG supply crunch. While the government announced a 20% allocation of requirements for commercial users, the Federation of Hotel & Restaurant Associations of India warned that small and medium outlets remain most vulnerable to high operational costs.

articles.headline: Ultraviolette introduces Battery-as-a-Service scheme to lower costs
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based Ultraviolette Automotive has partnered with Ecofy to offer its 'Battery Flex' financing program. The scheme allows buyers to pay a monthly subscription of ₹2,499 over five years, transferring battery ownership after the term. This initiative reduces the upfront purchase cost of Ultraviolette electric motorcycles by up to ₹1.5 lakh, aiming to improve EV affordability.

articles.headline: Innovision extends IPO deadline to March 17 amid lukewarm response
articles.summary: Innovision, a manpower and toll plaza management service provider, has extended its IPO closing date to March 17 and lowered its price band to ₹494-519 per share. The move follows a 32% overall subscription rate during the first three days. Originally set to close on March 12, the company revised the terms to attract more investor participation after initial interest remained subdued.

articles.headline: Balaji Amines shares fall on West Asia ammonia supply worries
articles.summary: Shares of Balaji Amines dropped over 4% as the West Asia conflict disrupted the supply of ammonia and liquefied natural gas. Ammonia is a critical raw material for manufacturing methylamines and ethylamines. The company informed stock exchanges that it faces severe logistical disruptions, and the full financial and operational impact cannot be fully assessed at this stage.

articles.headline: Coal India arm CMPDIL to launch IPO on March 20
articles.summary: Central Mine Planning and Design Institute Limited (CMPDIL), a wholly owned subsidiary of Coal India, will launch its initial public offering on March 20. The issue is entirely an offer for sale of 10.71 crore shares. CMPDIL provides consultancy and exploration services for coal and lithium reserves. The proceeds from the sale will go directly to the promoter shareholder, Coal India.

articles.headline: Raajmarg Infra InvIT IPO subscribed 13.7 times on final day
articles.summary: The ₹6,000-crore initial public offering of National Highways Authority of India-sponsored Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust was subscribed 13.74 times. Institutional investors bid for 19.14 times their portion. The InvIT mopped up ₹1,728 crore from anchor investors including LIC and ICICI Prudential. The price band for the issue was fixed at ₹99-100 per unit.

articles.headline: India listed REIT market expands sixfold since FY20 to ₹1.7 lakh crore
articles.summary: India’s listed REIT market has grown from ₹27,100 crore in FY20 to ₹1,72,600 crore in the first nine months of FY26, according to a CBRE report. The surge is driven by fresh listings and rising unit prices. India now has five listed REITs. Analysts expect structural reforms to further expand the investor base and provide flexible exit options for key institutional participants.

articles.headline: Aurionpro Solutions bags ₹350 crore data centre contract
articles.summary: Enterprise technology infrastructure provider Aurionpro Solutions has secured a multi-year deal worth approximately ₹350 crore to set up a data centre for a hyperscale operator in southern India. The contract covers comprehensive design, engineering, and execution of mechanical, electrical, and plumbing works, marking a significant win for the company’s technology infrastructure business.

articles.headline: Domestic tractor sales jump 34% in February on rural demand
articles.summary: Tractor sales in India grew by 34% year-on-year in February, recording 78,901 units. Industry players attribute the growth to strong rural sentiment, a robust rabi sowing area, and improved farm economics following a favorable kharif harvest. Exports also increased by 7% during the same period. However, manufacturers are monitoring the potential impact of a predicted El Nino on future demand.

articles.headline: Rapido signals food delivery push with launch of Ownly platform
articles.summary: Urban mobility start-up Rapido has entered the food delivery market with 'Ownly', starting in Bengaluru. The launch comes as Rapido raises $550–600 million in a funding round led by Prosus. Unlike incumbents Swiggy and Zomato, Rapido plans to leverage its existing rider network and restaurant partnerships without heavy initial discounting to build a sustainable additional revenue stream.

articles.headline: CII releases coffee table book featuring Tamil Nadu CEOs
articles.summary: The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) has released a book titled 'Tamil Nadu CEOs', featuring insights from top industry leaders in the state. Released during the CEO CONFLUENCE 3.0, the book includes thoughts from heads of Indian Bank, Saint Gobain India, and Aachi Group. The event also featured discussions on navigating current geopolitical crises and India's strategic relations.

articles.headline: Panasonic targets 50% AC sales growth despite rising input costs
articles.summary: Panasonic India aims for 50% growth in air-conditioner sales this year, even as the industry prepares for 12-15% price hikes due to higher copper and aluminium costs. The company plans to bring premium energy-saving and connected features to mid and entry-level models. Panasonic is targeting annual sales of 1.1 million units as it seeks to double manufacturing capacity by FY28.

articles.headline: Mercedes and Audi plan price hikes of up to 2% from April
articles.summary: Luxury carmakers Mercedes-Benz and Audi will implement price corrections of up to 2% across their India model ranges from April 1. The hikes are attributed to sustained currency volatility, specifically the rupee’s depreciation against the euro, and rising input costs. Mercedes prices may increase by up to ₹7.33 lakh depending on the model, while Audi anticipates hikes of up to ₹4.64 lakh.

articles.headline: Tata Motors bags orders for over 5,000 buses from STUs
articles.summary: Tata Motors has secured cumulative orders for over 5,000 buses and bus chassis from multiple State Transport Undertakings (STUs), including those in Maharashtra, Gujarat, and Kerala. The orders, awarded through competitive e-bidding, include intercity and intra-city models. The company will also provide lifecycle maintenance support through its Sampoorna Seva 2.0 program.

articles.headline: Steel Cos Brace for Costly Inputs and Cheaper Imports
articles.summary: The domestic steel industry is facing a double crisis as the West Asia conflict drives up input costs and increases the risk of cheap imports from China, Japan, and ASEAN countries. Approximately 1.5 million tonnes of steel are currently seeking alternative markets, threatening to flood the Indian market as local production costs rise.

articles.headline: Urea Supply Crunch May Disrupt National Goods Movement
articles.summary: A disruption in the supply of technical grade urea, essential for producing diesel exhaust fluid (DEF) used in heavy commercial vehicles, threatens to halt India's goods transportation fleet. Most manufacturers have only a few weeks of inventory left as the West Asia crisis impacts maritime supply chains.

articles.headline: ST Telemedia and JSW Group in Talks for Vodafone Idea Stake
articles.summary: Singapore-based ST Telemedia and India's JSW Group are reportedly in discussions with Vodafone Idea (Vi) for a potential equity stake. The debt-laden telecom operator is seeking a strategic investor to bring in approximately $1 billion in fresh capital to fund its 5G rollout and network expansion.

articles.headline: India and China Hit by US Trade Probe into Forced Labour
articles.summary: The US has launched a trade investigation into 60 countries, including India and China, to determine if their policies fail to prevent goods produced with forced labour. The probe under Section 301 could lead to new trade barriers or tariffs on various Indian exports if unfair practices are identified.

articles.headline: Industrial Kitchens Switch to Electricity Amid LPG Rationing
articles.summary: Manufacturing plants and commercial kitchens across India are switching to electricity and alternative fuels as the West Asia conflict disrupts LPG supplies. Industrial units in sectors like automobiles and food processing have reported gas shortages, forcing them to modify cooking and heating processes to maintain operations.

articles.headline: India Passenger Vehicle Sales Reach Record High in February
articles.summary: Indian passenger vehicle sales hit a record high in February, driven by tax cuts and strong demand for utility vehicles. According to SIAM data, the industry saw robust growth across segments, with vehicle dispatches rising 4-6% as manufacturers capitalised on improved supply chains and positive consumer sentiment.

articles.headline: Heineken Targets India as World's Largest Untapped Beer Market
articles.summary: Global brewer Heineken is betting on India's shifting social attitudes and rising incomes to unlock what it calls the world's largest untapped beer market. The company, which owns United Breweries, plans to expand its premium portfolio and increase its market share as urban consumption patterns evolve.

articles.headline: Legacy Automakers Scale Back EV Investments as Startups Struggle
articles.summary: Major automakers like Honda and GM are scaling back their electric vehicle investment plans as consumer demand slows and startups like Rivian and Lucid face financial challenges. Companies are increasingly focusing on hybrid models and cost-effective EVs as high interest rates and charging infrastructure gaps persist.

articles.headline: Sebi Allows Intraday Borrowings for Mutual Funds to Settle Redemptions
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) has allowed mutual funds to borrow intraday to manage cash flow mismatches during periods of heavy redemptions. The new rules allow funds to borrow up to 20% of their net assets for a maximum of six months to ensure they can meet investor payout requirements without disruption.

articles.headline: Vedanta Limited Raises ₹2,575 Crore to Refinance Existing Debt
articles.summary: Vedanta Limited successfully raised ₹2,575 crore through a three-year bond sale to refinance its existing debt and manage its high interest obligations. The bond issue, which carries a coupon rate of 8.75%, was well-received by institutional investors despite the ongoing volatility in the domestic credit market.

articles.headline: Muthoot FinCorp to Raise ₹600 Crore via Retail Bond Issue
articles.summary: Gold loan provider Muthoot FinCorp plans to raise up to ₹600 crore through a public issue of secured non-convertible debentures (NCDs). The funds will be used to expand the company's lending portfolio and meet general corporate requirements as demand for gold-backed loans remains strong in rural and semi-urban areas.

articles.headline: Social Media Memes Desensitize Public Perception of Massive Corporate Layoffs
articles.summary: Corporate layoffs have become a recurring theme for internet memes, changing how job loss is processed culturally. Recent staff cuts at companies like Block and Amazon were followed by waves of humorous content. While humor helps victims cope with the stigma of job loss, critics argue that the trend inadvertently desensitizes the public to the human impact of unemployment.

articles.headline: Demand for Rare Trophy Watches Remains Robust Despite Luxury Market Slowdown
articles.summary: While the broader luxury watch market has flattened, rare timepieces continue to fetch high prices at auctions. Christie's watch sales totaled $157 million in 2025. Collectors are increasingly seeking 'trophy watches' with unique provenance, such as double-signed dials or independent case makers. India is emerging as a growth market, though participation in global auctions remains limited.

articles.headline: Supertails Co-Founder Vineet Khanna on Building India’s Pet Support Ecosystem
articles.summary: Vineet Khanna, co-founder of the ₹500-crore brand Supertails, discusses the challenges of pet parenting in India. The platform provides food, healthcare, and tele-consultations to 700,000 users. Using data on 500,000 pet birthdays and breeds, the company personalizes care for a market where young Gen Z owners are shifting from being 'pet parents' to 'pet siblings'.

articles.headline: Jindal Steel’s Bid for Thyssenkrupp Assets Hits Deadlock Over Liabilities
articles.summary: Negotiations between Jindal Steel International and Germany’s Thyssenkrupp AG have stalled over pension liabilities and fiscal support requirements. Jindal is seeking financial commitments from the German government to fund the transition to cleaner electric arc furnaces. Thyssenkrupp has requested transparency on Jindal’s post-acquisition investment and financing plans.

articles.headline: Sebi Decision to End Goal-Based Mutual Funds Leaves ₹57,000 Crore Uncertainty
articles.summary: A Sebi directive to discontinue solution-oriented mutual fund schemes like retirement and children's education funds has impacted over 6 million folios. Existing schemes must stop subscriptions and merge with other funds having similar asset profiles. Investors are concerned about the loss of mandatory lock-in periods that helped maintain long-term financial discipline.

articles.headline: West Asia Conflict Risks Wiping Out $3 Billion in Phone Exports
articles.summary: India's mobile phone exports, which reached $11 billion in the first half of the fiscal, are threatened by the prolonged Iran-Israel war. Analysts estimate a $2-3 billion loss as the Gulf and West Asia regions, which account for 12% of net electronics exports, face economic disruption. Contract manufacturers like Foxconn and Tata Electronics may need to reroute shipments to other regions.

articles.headline: Domestic Car Dispatches to Dealers Rise for Fifth Straight Month
articles.summary: February data from the Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers (Siam) shows a fifth consecutive month of rising car sales to dealers. The growth was supported by recent tax cuts. However, Siam officials noted that the ongoing conflict in West Asia remains a significant concern for the industry's future performance and supply chain stability.

articles.headline: Government Set to Scrap IDBI Bank Sale Over Unviable Bid Offers
articles.summary: India is likely to halt the disinvestment process of IDBI Bank as the bids received were below the government’s minimum reserve price. This brings a technical stop to the high-profile sale of a majority stake in the bank. Officials declined to quantify the specific bids or the reserve price, but noted that current offers do not meet government expectations.

articles.headline: Sebi Finalizes Framework for Intraday Borrowing by Mutual Fund Schemes
articles.summary: The market regulator has formalized rules allowing mutual funds to borrow money intraday without an upper limit to manage liquidity mismatches. The borrowing must be vetted by AMC boards and trustees and disclosed on websites. The framework aims to help funds manage T+1 redemption payouts when maturity proceeds from other instruments are received later in the day.

articles.headline: Premium Personal Care Products Outpace Mass Segment Growth for FMCG Firms
articles.summary: Major FMCG companies like Marico, Hindustan Unilever, and Dabur are seeing double-digit growth in premium and digital-first brands. Specialized haircare and skincare portfolios are scaling faster than mass-market products, helping support margins amid uneven demand. HUL's premium and D2C brands OZiva and Minimalist now have an annual run rate of ₹1,100 crore.

articles.headline: Mirae CEO Swarup Mohanty Warns of Historic Power Shift in Global Markets
articles.summary: Mirae Asset India CEO Swarup Mohanty states that markets are entering a unique juncture where the US and China are reshaping the geopolitical order. He highlighted that oil and dollar dynamics are changing global dominance patterns. Mohanty expects Indian markets to recover by the third quarter of 2026 once corporate earnings align with macroeconomic fundamentals.

articles.headline: Sitharaman announces Rs 1 lakh crore Economic Stabilisation Fund
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman announced a plan to set up a Rs 1 lakh crore Economic Stabilisation Fund. The fund aims to provide fiscal headroom to respond to unanticipated global challenges and supply chain disruptions. It will support sectors vulnerable to West Asia tensions and volatile commodity prices, ensuring domestic market stability.

articles.headline: Steel companies cut output due to gas supply shortages
articles.summary: Major Indian steel manufacturers, including JSW and Tata Steel, have announced production cuts of up to 20% due to a shortage of natural gas. The supply chain has been hit by the ongoing conflict in West Asia, which has disrupted LNG imports. Companies are exploring alternative fuels, but warn that prolonged shortages could lead to an increase in steel prices.

articles.headline: NCERT review row exposes systemic gaps in textbook approval process
articles.summary: A controversy over a Class 8 textbook chapter on judicial corruption has highlighted flaws in NCERT’s approval system. Reporting shows the 19-member National Syllabus and Teaching-Learning Material Committee (NSTC) did not explicitly sign off on the draft, which was shared via email and WhatsApp. Systemic issues were flagged after the section on corruption was explicitly contested.

articles.headline: IIT Gandhinagar ends ties with Michel Danino over textbook controversy
articles.summary: IIT Gandhinagar discontinued its association with guest professor Michel Danino following a Supreme Court order. Danino was part of a committee that drafted a withdrawn NCERT Class 8 textbook containing a section on judicial corruption. The court ordered public institutions to dissociate from the authors involved in the controversial academic curriculum.

articles.headline: IIT Delhi first-year student found dead on campus
articles.summary: A 19-year-old first-year BTech student at IIT Delhi allegedly died by suicide in his hostel room. No suicide note was found, but initial investigations suggest the student was struggling with academic pressure and personal issues. The incident has sparked fresh calls for better mental health support and counseling services within premier educational institutions.

articles.headline: Investigation reveals Banksy's identity as Robin Gunningham using alias David Jones
articles.summary: A Reuters investigation has traced the identity of the street artist Banksy to Robin Gunningham, who legally changed his name to David Jones. The probe used immigration records from a 2022 trip to Ukraine and a 2000 New York arrest confession. The investigation also identified Robert Del Naja of Massive Attack as a frequent secret painting partner.

articles.headline: Religious community kitchens continue langar services despite fuel crisis
articles.summary: Free community kitchens at large temples and gurudwaras are maintaining operations despite LPG rationalisation. Many major sites, such as the Golden Temple and Dakshineswar Kali Temple, use combinations of PNG, firewood, and electricity. However, smaller religious organisations dependent solely on LPG cylinders have sought government intervention to ensure uninterrupted fuel supplies.

articles.headline: Entourage Costs Exceed 10% of Total Bollywood Film Budgets
articles.summary: Film producers in Mumbai are struggling with rising costs as the remuneration for stars' personal staff, including security and stylists, now exceeds 10% of total production budgets. These 'entourage' fees are often higher than the salaries of key technical crew members like cinematographers and writers.

articles.headline: Musicians Turned Politicians Experiment with Celebrity and Dissent Globally
articles.summary: Musicians like Nepal’s rapper Balen Shah, punk artist Jello Biafra, and Uganda’s Bobi Wine have transitioned from music to mainstream politics. Balen Shah recently secured a landslide victory in Nepal’s parliamentary elections, defeating former PM K.P. Sharma Oli. These artists use social media and anti-establishment sentiment to challenge traditional political structures and elites.

articles.headline: Indian Organizations Build Contemporary Archives to Preserve History of Modern India
articles.summary: Companies and institutions like Godrej, Ashoka University, and the National Centre for Biological Sciences are creating archives of everyday objects and papers. These repositories include manufacturing drawings, private letters of former PMs, and scientific equipment. The movement aims to document India’s journey in science, feminism, consumerism, and activism for future research.

articles.headline: Mumbai Art Show Strangers in Land Explores Drawings from 1970s-80s
articles.summary: The Subcontinent gallery in Mumbai is presenting 'Strangers in Land', an exhibition featuring the works of Anjana Mehra and V.R. Patel. The show highlights intricate graphic skills and surrealistic scenes from decades past. Patel, an enigmatic figure in Indian art, focused on private drawing practices. The exhibition is open at the Fort-based gallery until April 12, 2026.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Museum Showcases Intertwined History of Botany and British Empire
articles.summary: The Museum of Art & Photography in Bengaluru is hosting 'Paper Gardens', an institutional survey featuring 120 works of rare botanical imagery from the 17th to 20th centuries. Curated by Shrey Maurya, the exhibition explores the global circuits of botanical exchange and the specific history of Lalbagh. It features works produced by master artists for East India Company officers.

articles.headline: Dance Performance Lori Highlights the Suffering of Children in Gaza War
articles.summary: Indian Dancers for Gaza’s Children (IDGC) performed 'Lori', a piece based on Faiz Ahmed Faiz’s poem 'A Lullaby For A Palestinian Child'. Recited by Shabana Azmi, the performance aims to raise funds and awareness for children living through the conflict. The global collective includes over 900 members across 32 countries using art to inspire compassion for war victims.

articles.headline: Scholars Revisit Edward Said’s Critique of Media Reporting on Islam
articles.summary: Analyst Somak Ghoshal explores how Edward Said’s 1981 study 'Covering Islam' remains relevant during the current US-Iran war. The critique argues that Western media often simplifies Islamic nations into 'oil suppliers' or 'potential terrorists'. This skewed framing justifies foreign intervention and ignores the nuanced perspectives of the people living within those countries.

articles.headline: The Word Intentional Becomes Overused Cliché in Post-Pandemic Wellness Culture
articles.summary: The adjective 'intentional' has moved from corporate leadership workshops to a ubiquitous lifestyle label for dating, parenting, and design. Linguistics experts argue that the word is losing its meaning due to lazy repetition on social media. Originally rooted in the Latin 'intendere' for focused attention, it is now used as a hollow shortcut to signal mindfulness.

articles.headline: History of Persian Language in India Highlights Legacy of Cultural Exchange
articles.summary: Historian Manu S. Pillai traces the decline of Persian as India's lingua franca, noting its replacement by English during the colonial era. For centuries, Persian was the language of diplomacy, poetry, and administration across Muslim and Hindu courts. The transition is illustrated by 19th-century royal portraits shifting from Persian robes to Western attire, marking a new political age.

articles.headline: New Book Glorious Failure Challenges Myths of French Colonialism in India
articles.summary: Robert Ivermee’s new history book, 'Glorious Failure', examines the French presence in India and its challenge to British dominance. The text uncovers the dark history of French involvement in the Indian Ocean slave trade, linked to traffickers in Senegal and Guinea. It contests the popular notion of the French as a benign, exotic presence, focusing on mercantile and missionary malpractices.

articles.headline: Indian Chefs and Mixologists Spotlighting Local Seaweed Species in Menus
articles.summary: Local seaweed varieties like sargassum and ulva are gaining popularity in Indian fine dining for their umami and sustainability. Startups like MariTide and The Good Ocean are scaling supply chains to provide fresh and dehydrated seaweed to top restaurants. Chefs are using the ingredient in everything from mushroom risotto and bhel to cocktails and miso, replacing imported varieties.

articles.headline: Delhi Exhibition Uses Kantha Embroidery to Revive and Reimagine Old Textiles
articles.summary: Designers and artisans in Delhi are showcasing 'The Kantha Project', an exhibition focused on upcycling tattered fabrics through traditional Bengal embroidery. The show features a restored 2013 Kalamkari painting and intricate threadwork on jamdani and shibori fabrics. The project aims to evolve the craft beyond traditional quilts to attract contemporary consumers and ensure its survival.

articles.headline: New Book and Interactive Website Document Artistic Complexity of Ellora Caves
articles.summary: A new publication and geolocated website, Ellora: A Cross-Fertilization of Style, explores the 34 rock-cut temples in Maharashtra. The project geolocates 7,000 images and floor plans to show the relationships between Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain imagery. Research highlights how sculptor guilds moved between sites under different royal patrons, taking artistic liberties with religious myths.

articles.headline: Brazilian Film The Secret Agent Confronts Dictatorship and Culture of Forgetting
articles.summary: Director Kleber Mendonça Filho’s Oscar-nominated film uses the spy genre to recount the military dictatorship in Brazil during the late 1970s. The movie follows a professor in hiding and serves as a preservation tool against 'self-inflicted amnesia' regarding political violence and disappeared dissidents. It frames cinema as a vital document of truth against rewritten state histories.

articles.headline: Three arrested with 2kg of explosives in Haryana’s Ambala district
articles.summary: Haryana’s Special Task Force (STF) arrested three men near Barara for possessing nearly 2kg of explosive material. The trio was intercepted on a motorcycle following a tip-off. Police are investigating the source and intended use of the explosives.

articles.headline: Karnataka hotels face 10-day wait for commercial LPG cylinders
articles.summary: The Karnataka government is prioritizing LPG supply for hospitals, hostels, and residential schools, leaving hotels with a potential 10-day wait. Minister KH Muniyappa advised hoteliers to use electric stoves temporarily. The state is monitoring fuel stations to prevent black marketing of Auto LPG as shipments from the Strait of Hormuz remain delayed.

articles.headline: LPG theft incidents rise in Karnataka districts amid supply shortage
articles.summary: Authorities in Yadgir and Ramanagara have intensified raids to curb the illegal use and theft of LPG. In Yadgir, 46 subsidized cylinders were seized from commercial establishments. In Ramanagara, two men were arrested for stealing eight cylinders from a delivery vehicle. Forest officials have also been put on alert to prevent tree felling for firewood fuel.

articles.headline: Losses in ‘digital arrest’ scams in Karnataka reach ₹468 crore
articles.summary: Karnataka residents have lost approximately ₹468 crore to "digital arrest" frauds over the last three years, with 1,695 cases registered. Home Minister G Parameshwara noted that even technically skilled individuals are falling victim. The government plans to establish cybercrime police stations in every district and continues to partner with Infosys to strengthen enforcement.

articles.headline: Three arrested for Dalit man's murder in Bagalkot district
articles.summary: Police arrested three individuals for the murder of 19-year-old Sudeep Basappa Pange in Savalagai village. The victim was allegedly attacked and subjected to casteist slurs following a dispute over stone-throwing at a school cultural event. The accused have been booked under the BNS and the SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act.

articles.headline: Three dead in car-bus collision in Dakshina Kannada district
articles.summary: A car colliding with a KSRTC bus near Shiradi Ghat resulted in three fatalities and one serious injury. The victims were travelling from Hassan to Mangaluru. Uppinangady police have registered a case and are investigating the accident.

articles.headline: Tribal man rescued from 30 years of bonded labour in Udupi
articles.summary: Police in Udupi arrested a woman for allegedly forcing a 65-year-old Koraga community member to work as a bonded labourer for nearly three decades.
