The victim was denied wages, forced to live in a cattle shed, and subjected to physical and mental abuse. The accused was charged under the SC/ST Act and the Bonded Labour System (Abolition) Act.

articles.headline: Five killed in suspected hooch tragedy in Bihar’s Saran district
articles.summary: At least five people died after consuming suspected illicit liquor in Saran, Bihar. The deaths have reignited debate over the state's prohibition policy. Local authorities are conducting an investigation to identify the source of the spurious alcohol.

articles.headline: Crisis leaves 23,000 Indian seafarers stranded across Gulf region
articles.summary: The West Asia conflict has affected nearly 23,000 Indian seafarers, with 768 currently on vessels stuck near the Persian Gulf. The Directorate General of Shipping reported seven maritime incidents resulting in three fatalities. Communications remain difficult due to internet unavailability in some areas. A 24-hour control room has been set up to coordinate assistance and evacuations.

articles.headline: College campuses trim menus amid ongoing LPG supply crisis
articles.summary: Educational institutions like IIT-Madras and VIT Vellore are adjusting hostel dining menus as LPG availability becomes uncertain. Some campuses have switched to firewood or electric cooktops to ensure student meals continue uninterrupted during exams. The Indian School of Business and other private institutions are also monitoring the situation and rationalising menus to handle the crunch.

articles.headline: Two Indians killed in Oman drone debris incident
articles.summary: Two Indian expatriate workers were killed and 10 others injured when debris from a downed drone fell in an industrial area in the Sohar province of Oman. The Indian mission is in close touch with local authorities and the concerned company to provide assistance and repatriate the mortal remains. Separately, efforts are underway to rescue 15 crew members from the attacked Safesea Vishnu.

articles.headline: India-Gulf Flight Services Gradually Resume as Airspace Reopens
articles.summary: Airlines have begun slowly resuming flight services between India and major Gulf hubs like Dubai and Abu Dhabi as regional airspace restrictions are eased. While flight frequencies remain below normal levels, the resumption is expected to help thousands of stranded passengers and restore critical business travel links.

articles.headline: Indian Professionals in West Asia Consider Returning Home Amid Crisis
articles.summary: Many Indian professionals working in West Asia are considering returning to India as the regional security situation deteriorates. Recruitment firms have reported a 25-30% increase in inquiries from NRIs looking for jobs in India, citing safety concerns and the high cost of living in conflict zones as primary reasons.

articles.headline: Pet Owners Urged to Include Animals in Emergency Disaster Planning
articles.summary: Veterinary experts emphasize the need for including pets in emergency plans as animals are frequently abandoned during crises and conflicts. Key recommendations include permanent microchip identification, transport readiness via crate training, and maintaining digital vaccination records. Support networks and temporary care sheets are also cited as vital for ensuring animal safety.

articles.headline: Drone strike in Oman kills five Indians
articles.summary: Two Indian nationals were killed and 10 others injured in a drone strike on an industrial area in Oman's Sohar province. This brings the total number of Indian fatalities in recent regional strikes to five. The Indian Embassy is coordinating with Omani authorities to provide medical assistance and repatriate the bodies of the deceased.

articles.headline: Meta delays rollout of ‘Avocado’ AI model to May due to performance
articles.summary: Meta has postponed the release of its new artificial intelligence model, codenamed "Avocado," from March to at least May. Reports suggest the model has fallen short in performance compared to Google’s Gemini rivals. The company continues to invest heavily, with a planned capital expenditure of up to $135 billion for 2026 to pursue "superintelligence."

articles.headline: VinFast VF6 electric SUV review: Strong performance vs practicality
articles.summary: The VinFast VF6, the brand's entry-level model for India, offers a 59.6-kWh battery and a range of 463-468 km. While the cabin features premium finishes and Level 2 ADAS, the vehicle faces challenges in backseat legroom and boot space. Priced between ₹17.29 lakh and ₹19.19 lakh, it delivers impressive ride quality and handling but misses a spare wheel and retractable glass roof shade.

articles.headline: KTM 390 Adventure R debuts as hardcore off-road variant
articles.summary: KTM has launched the 390 Adventure R, priced at ₹3.77 lakh. It features 21-/18-inch tubed tyres, 272 mm ground clearance, and 230 mm suspension travel. The bike is powered by a 399 cc engine producing 45.3 bhp. Designed for committed off-road riders, it includes adjustable suspension and various rider aids, though its tall seat height and tubed tyres may challenge some users.

articles.headline: Renault unveils Bridger compact SUV concept for Indian market
articles.summary: Renault has revealed the Bridger concept, a boxy compact SUV slated to debut in India by late 2027. Positioned between the Kiger and Duster, it will feature 18-inch wheels and 200 mm ground clearance. Built on the R-GMP platform, the model will offer petrol, hybrid, and EV powertrain options, making it a versatile contender in the rugged visual appeal SUV segment.

articles.headline: QJMotor rolls out SRK 421 RR supersport motorcycle
articles.summary: Chinese brand QJMotor has introduced the SRK 421 RR, powered by a 421cc inline-four engine producing 77 bhp. The bike features Marzocchi adjustable suspension, Brembo brakes, and multi-level traction control. Weighing 191 kg, it boasts a futuristic design. While its launch in India is currently unlikely, it is positioned to compete with high-end models like the Kawasaki Ninja ZX-4R.

articles.headline: Explainer: Why Shahed kamikaze drones are decisive in modern warfare
articles.summary: Shahed drones, or 'kamikaze drones', are unmanned aircraft that detonate upon hitting targets. Their low cost allows them to be sent in swarms, overwhelming expensive air defence systems. Primarily associated with Iran, they gained notoriety during the 2022 invasion of Ukraine. Indian startups like Zuppa and IG Defence are reportedly working on indigenous versions optimized for high-altitude use.

articles.headline: Apple lowers China App Store commission fees to 25%
articles.summary: Apple announced it will reduce commission fees for in-app purchases in mainland China from 30% to 25% starting Sunday. The fee for small businesses and mini-apps will be cut to 12%. The decision follows pressure from Chinese regulators in Apple’s second-largest market. The move is seen as a significant win for local developers seeking better margins on digital transactions.

articles.headline: Nothing Begins Exporting India-Made Smartphones to US Market
articles.summary: Consumer tech brand Nothing has started exporting its India-manufactured smartphones to the United States. The Chennai facility, operated by BYD, is now producing sets for 48 global markets, as the company aims to capitalise on the growing demand for its transparent-design mobile devices.

articles.headline: TRAI Proposes Termination Charges on A2P SMS to Stop Spam
articles.summary: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) has proposed introducing termination charges on Application-to-Person (A2P) messages to curb the rising menace of spam and fraudulent calls. The move is designed to make bulk spamming more expensive for businesses and protect consumers from unwanted commercial communication.

articles.headline: Elon Musk to Rebuild xAI as Co-Founder Haotian Liu Departs
articles.summary: Elon Musk has pledged to rebuild his artificial intelligence startup xAI following the departure of co-founder Haotian Liu, who has joined rival firm Anthropic. The exit marks another high-profile departure from xAI as the company struggles to compete with established players like OpenAI and Google in the AI race.

articles.headline: Nvidia to Unveil New AI Chips at Annual GTC Conference
articles.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is expected to unveil the company’s next generation of AI-powered chips and software at the upcoming GTC conference in San Jose. Investors are looking for updates on the new Blackwell architecture, which is expected to cement Nvidia's lead in the global AI hardware market.

articles.headline: AI Integration Poised to Reboot the Decade-Old Smart Speaker Market
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is expected to transform voice assistants from simple timers into conversational home managers. Amazon's new Echo Show 8, featuring the AZ3 Pro chip, hints at a future of edge processing where tasks happen locally rather than in the cloud. New AI-powered versions like Alexa+ and Gemini aim to deliver the context-aware experience promised a decade ago.

articles.headline: Trai Proposes New Penalties for Telecom Operators Facilitating Bulk Spam Calls
articles.summary: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India has proposed a 5 paise per minute termination charge on operators whose networks originate robocalls from non-standard numbers. The draft regulation also suggests financial penalties for improper registration of SMS headers. The move aims to hold telecom companies accountable for the surge in automated unsolicited commercial communications.

articles.headline: Shantanu Narayen to exit Adobe after 18 years
articles.summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen has announced he will step down after nearly two decades leading the software giant. Narayen’s tenure saw Adobe transition to a subscription-based model and more recently, integrate generative AI across its creative suite. His exit comes as the company navigates intense competition in the AI space. A successor has not yet been named.

articles.headline: Australian report: 1/5th of teens bypass social media ban
articles.summary: Despite a national ban on social media for children under 16, nearly 20% of Australian teenagers continue to use platforms like TikTok and Instagram using VPNs or false age data. The eSafety Commissioner's report highlights the difficulty of enforcing digital age restrictions. The government is considering stricter identity verification measures for tech companies.

articles.headline: Bharat Biotech marks 30 years as global vaccine leader
articles.summary: Hyderabad-based Bharat Biotech celebrated its 30th anniversary by unveiling a commemorative logo. Founded in Genome Valley, the company has delivered over 9 billion vaccine doses to 125 countries, covering diseases like Covid-19, Rotavirus, and Malaria. Co-founder Krishna Ella noted that the firm started with a belief that Indian science could solve global health challenges.

articles.headline: Diabetic foot cases on the rise in Karnataka
articles.summary: Health experts have warned of a sharp increase in diabetic foot cases in Karnataka, with nearly 13,000 cases reported in the last year. High blood sugar can cause nerve damage (neuropathy) and poor circulation, leading to severe infections and amputations. Doctors recommend daily foot inspections and wearing specialized offloading footwear to prevent complications.

articles.headline: Improved seeds and technology boost domestic wheat and rice output
articles.summary: India's wheat and rice production rose in 2024-25, aided by climate-resilient seed varieties and better farming practices. Wheat output was estimated at 117.95 million tonnes, while rice reached 150.18 million tonnes. Despite record pool stocks, West Asia tensions have disrupted basmati exports due to higher freight costs and cargo re-routing, prompting close government monitoring.

articles.headline: TN CM Inaugurates ₹13,077 Cr Udangudi Thermal Plant, Other Projects
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM MK Stalin virtually inaugurated the first 660 MW unit of the ₹13,077-crore Udangudi Super Critical Thermal Power Plant in Tuticorin. This is India's first with a dedicated offshore captive port, aiming to meet rising power demand via a new 400 kV substation. He also launched ₹2,000+ crore road and water supply projects across Coimbatore, Salem, and Tiruppur.

articles.headline: Mondo Duplantis Breaks Pole Vault World Record for 15th Time
articles.summary: Swedish pole vaulter Armand "Mondo" Duplantis, 26, broke his own world record for the 15th time, clearing 6.31 meters on his first attempt at the Mondo Classic in Uppsala. He described the feat as 'pure magic', using a stiffer pole and longer run-up. Duplantis will next compete at the World Indoor Championships in Poland, ahead of the European Championships.

articles.headline: George Russell Secures First Career Sprint Pole at Shanghai GP
articles.summary: Mercedes driver George Russell secured his first career sprint pole position for the season's inaugural sprint race at the Shanghai Grand Prix. He clocked 1:31.520, leading every phase and outperforming championship leader Lando Norris in challenging track conditions. Russell will start alongside teammate Kimi Antonelli, positioning Mercedes strongly as car performance improves.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Clears ₹1 Lakh Crore Economic Stabilisation Fund
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha cleared an Economic Stabilisation Fund, proposed by FM Nirmala Sitharaman, valued at ₹57,381.84 crore as part of supplementary grants, with some reports citing up to ₹1 lakh crore. It aims to provide fiscal headroom against global headwinds, supply chain disruptions, and West Asia war shocks, ensuring FY26 expenditure stays within budget without increasing fiscal deficit.

articles.headline: Fitch Raises India's FY26 GDP Growth Forecast to 7.5%
articles.summary: Fitch Ratings upgraded India's FY26 GDP growth forecast to 7.5%, citing resilient domestic demand, strong investment, and government capital expenditure. This is up from previous estimates ranging 6.7%-7.4%. Fitch also revised FY27 growth to 6.7% but warned a prolonged oil price spike above $100/barrel due to the Iran war poses a significant adverse risk to global supply chain and inflation.

articles.headline: Rupee Hits Record Low of 92.45 Amid Oil Surge, Geopolitical Tensions
articles.summary: The Indian rupee slumped to a new lifetime low, settling at 92.45 against the US dollar, falling 20 paise and 72 paise over the week. The decline was fueled by Brent crude prices exceeding $101/barrel, sustained foreign fund outflows, and intensified geopolitical tensions from the West Asia war. Despite RBI intervention, aggressive dollar demand from importers is expected to keep the rupee under pressure.

articles.headline: India's Forex Reserves Drop $11.7 Billion Amid Volatility
articles.summary: India’s foreign exchange reserves saw one of its largest weekly declines, dropping by $11.68 billion to $716.81 billion for the week ended March 6. This significant fall from a previous all-time high of $728.494 billion is primarily attributed to the RBI's intervention to defend the rupee, coupled with high volatility from the West Asia war, a strengthening US dollar, and falling foreign currency assets.

articles.headline: Rupee Hits New Record Low of 92.48 Amid Oil Surge, Capital Outflows
articles.summary: The Indian Rupee plummeted to a new record low of 92.48 against the US dollar on Friday, falling 27 paise. This was driven by surging crude oil prices, heavy capital outflows from foreign investors, regional instability, and a strengthening dollar. Despite RBI interventions, the decline raises concerns about imported inflation and the widening trade deficit, with analysts predicting further volatility.

articles.headline: IndiGo Imposes Fuel Surcharge Up to ₹2,300 from March 14
articles.summary: IndiGo has introduced new fuel surcharges, effective March 14, ranging from ₹425 for domestic flights to ₹2,300 for long-haul Europe routes. This move, following Air India, aims to offset surging aviation turbine fuel prices and rising operational costs, especially due to the West Asian conflict, impacting both domestic and international tickets.

articles.headline: Shantanu Narayen Steps Down as Adobe CEO After 18 Years
articles.summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen is stepping down after 18 years, having transformed the company into a SaaS giant with revenue growing from $3.58B to $23.8B. He will remain Chairman as the board conducts a global search among internal and external candidates for his successor. Narayen led Adobe through the 2008 crisis and cloud shift but faces increasing AI competition; company shares dropped 6%.

articles.headline: Godrej Properties Promoters Hike Stake by 5%, Invest ₹2,674 Crore
articles.summary: Godrej Properties' promoters have increased their stake by 5% to 51.67% in FY26, investing ₹2,674 crore through open market purchases. This reflects their strong confidence in the company's growth prospects, robust residential demand, and launch pipeline. Godrej Properties was the largest listed realty firm last fiscal, with pre-sales nearing ₹30,000 crore.

articles.headline: HFCL Secures $1.1 Billion Global Optical Fibre Contract
articles.summary: HFCL Ltd. has secured a five-year contract worth $1.1 billion (₹10,159 crore) with a global entity to supply high-fibre-count optical fibre cables, primarily for data centre use. Running from 2026-2028 with extensions to 2030, this is one of the largest export deals for an Indian telecom company, reinforcing India's role as a global digital infrastructure manufacturing hub.

articles.headline: India to Ban Added Sugar in Baby Foods for Infants Aged 6-24 Months
articles.summary: FSSAI is drafting new standards to ban added sugar (sucrose, fructose) in baby food products for infants aged 6-24 months, permitting only naturally occurring lactose. This "zero-added sugar" standard aims to combat rising childhood obesity and metabolic diseases, with 83 million obese children predicted by 2035. The rule will apply to all manufacturers and imported products in India's $1.16-billion baby food market.
